title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
During a legal proceeding regarding the death of Andrew Vance, the district attorney, Krümmit, reveals that Vance was an American citizen who had previously lived in Pittsburgh, where his records identified him as an Armenian born in 1885. Elijah considers the implications of Vance's Armenian background. The prosecution also discusses Clinton, Vance's servant, who was an orphan raised at St. Vincent's Orphanage in Pittsburgh. Krümmit describes Clinton as a simple-minded, gentle, and respectful man. This is supported by the grocer, Bernheim, who describes Clinton as good-natured and exceptionally strong, capable of lifting sugar barrels.

Mayor and banker Matt Hollis testifies that Vance was a hardworking, quiet man who lived in a house rented from the Mayor. Hollis notes that Vance requested strange philosophical and historical books from Pittsburgh and kept his salary in cash at home rather than in the bank. The sheriff testifies that a search of Vance's house on December 25th revealed no money, and the marshal presents an empty, deformed green tin box used by Vance to store his funds. The postmaster testifies that Vance received very little mail and had not sent any letters in several months.

Dr. Strong, the pathologist, testifies that Vance likely died around midnight on Christmas Eve from a blow to the head or a gunshot wound. He notes that the body had nail holes in the hands and feet and that the head was severed with a very sharp tool after death. The coroner, Stapleton, presents a long-handled axe found in Vance's kitchen. The weapon, which belonged to the deceased and was typically used by the missing Clark, bore no fingerprints, suggesting the killer wore gloves or cleaned the blade.
